content:
# Direct Mode fp16 + Larger Batch — Design Decision (2026-04-10)

## What
Two stacked optimizations targeting the GPU stage of direct mode:
1. fp16 RAUNE inference via model.half()
2. Default batch_size 4 → 8 with new --direct-batch-size CLI flag

## Why
- After 3-thread pipeline (#65), GPU stage at 398ms/frame is the bottleneck
- User constraint: quality-preserving optimizations only (no proxy reduction, no frame skipping)
- fp16: ~2× on Ampere fp16 throughput, sub-1/255 numerical noise, free on RTX 3060
- Larger batch: ~10-20% from amortizing per-call overhead and SM occupancy
- Stack cleanly: fp16 halves activation memory, freeing room for larger batch

## Expected Outcome
- 2.47 fps → ~5 fps (combined target)
- Visually equivalent output (ΔE invisible)
- VRAM: similar to current (fp16 halves activations, batch=8 doubles them)

## Decomposition
This is PR 1 of 3 in the GPU sweep (issue #66):
- PR 1 (this): fp16 + batch — trivial wins, ~5 fps target
- PR 2 (follow-up): torch.compile(model.half()) — JIT fusion on top of fp16
- PR 3 (follow-up): NVDEC hardware decode — eliminates ~34ms decode

## Issue
chunzhe10/dorea#66

## Spec
docs/decisions/2026-04-10-direct-mode-fp16-batch.md
